content_of_post_to_comment: One of my father’s favourite tactics was making me afraid of the outside world. He was constantly talking about economic collapse, people who would hurt me, men who would abuse me, etc. To this day, I have not met someone in my life who has been more abusive than my father. It turns out the world was far safer than my home was.
comment: When 'outside danger' is constantly emphasized, it can shrink your threat map so the real risk at home becomes harder to name or compare against, because the mind calibrates safety relatively, not absolutely.
